other_findings.finding: T cells (including CD8+ T cells) emerge in prenatal skin later in gestation, accompanying thymus, bone marrow and spleen formation from around 10 PCW.
Single-cell alpha-beta TCR sequencing data were generated to accurately resolve T cell subsets in prenatal skin.
B cells and T cells emerged later in gestation compared to innate immune cells such as macrophages and ILCs, which were present from early gestation.
Pre-dermal condensate (pre-Dc) cells co-located with dendritic and lymphoid cells based on correlation analyses.

evidence_quotes.quote: Innate immune cells, such as macrophages and innate lymphoid cells (ILCs), were present from early gestation, whereas B cells and T cells emerged later, accompanying thymus, bone marrow and spleen formation from around 10 PCW.
evidence_quotes.context: Temporal dynamics of immune cell populations in prenatal skin

evidence_quotes.quote: Single-cell alpha-beta T cell receptor (TCR) sequencing data were generated to accurately resolve T cell subsets.
